- [Chapters 3 and 4: Hwang, K (2017) Cloud Computing for Machine Learning and Cognitive Applications, MIT Press.](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/1991124)

# Book

# Main

## Market-oriented public cloud

How is a balance and competition maintained

How is pricing worked out?

How do providers ensure good quality of service?

Consumers rely on Quality of Service(QoS) offered by providers

QoS that's negotiated is part of a Service Level Agreement(SLA)

![An illustration of the cloud market as described in the text.](data:image/png;base64,)

A cloud broker acts as an intermediary between cloud consumers and providers. Integration of cloud services may be too complex for consumers, so brokers can provide such services like

- Negotiate and integrate cloud services
- Move applications between different cloud vendors
- Reduce dependency on cloud provider
- Leverage volume discounts

Cloud providers

- AWS IaaS and PaaS
- Google Cloud Platform, PaaS and SaaS, like Gmail, Docs
- Microsoft Azure, PaaS and SaaS, live, sql, office 654
- IBM Smart Cloud, PaaS, SaaS IaaS

### Business models for cloud computing

Cloud computing is a stack formed by layers.

e.g.

- Application clouds - SaaS
- Platform Clouds - PaaS
- Compute/storage Clouds -IaaS
- Colocation Clouds - servers rented in a data centre
- Network Clouds - network capabilities and resources are hosted in a cloud, managed in-house or by a service provider and available on demand

Cloud providers can rent machines in a data centre or have their own centres.

### Virtualisation of cloud service

Virtual memory - SWAP

- Servers: multiple VM on one server. XenServer, PowerVM, Hyper-V, Vmware, EXS Server
- Desktops: Upgrade applications with flexibility, Vmware Workstation, Xen desktop, virtualpc
- Network: Virtual private networks(VPNs), virtual clusters, intranet virtualisation, openStack, Eucalyptus
- Storage: Network storage and NAS virtualisation for shared cluster or cloud applications, like Dropbox, Apple iCloud, AWS s3, OneDrive, IBM Datastore
- Application: Software process-level virtualisation like Docker, XenApp, MSCRM, SaaS Clouds

Virtualisation can occur at various levels

- Hardware - VM  - hyperviser type 1
- Of Operating system - Hosted VM - hyperviser type 2
- Within Operating system - Container

### Hardware virtualisation

- Full virtualisation
- Para-virtualisation

Full virtualisation - OS not modified and non-critical instruction run directly on the hardware. Critical instructions are discovered and replaced with trap into the virtualisation software to be emulated - called binary translation

Performance of virtualisation may not be ideal with to the binary translation, full virtualisation of I/O intensive application is a challenge.

![The concept of full virtualisation using a hypervisor sitting on top of the bare metal hardware in the host machine. Within the virtualisation layer (the hypervisor), sits two sets of applications and guest operating systems.](data:image/png;base64,)

![Indirect execution of complex instructions via binary translation of guest OS requests using the VMM layer along with direct execution of simple user instruction on the host machine. Ring 2 function is not used here. Ring 3 hosts the user apps, which via a direct execution of user request relates to the host computer system hardware. Ring 2’s function is empty, and the Ring 1 and Ring 0 functions host a guest OS and VMM respectively, which via a binary translation of OS request relates to the host computer system hardware.](data:image/png;base64,)

Or there is virtualisation layer like VMWare on the host OS, so there can be 4 layers of mapping making it quite slow

Para-virtualisation

Modifying the guest OS kernel to replace non-virtualizable instruction with hyper-calls for the hypervisor or VMM to carry out the virtualisation process. For I/O applications this can be preferable for performance purposes.

e.g. Xen, KVM, VMWare ESX

![Para-virtualisation involves modifying the guest OS kernel to replace non-virtualisable instructions with hypercalls for the hypervisor or the VMM to carry out the virtualisation process.](data:image/png;base64,)

![Para-virtualisation involves modifying the guest OS kernel to replace non-virtualisable instructions with hypercalls for the hypervisor or the VMM to carry out the virtualisation process. This diagram shows two sets of applications, this time with a para-virtualised guest operating system each, which then fits into a hypervisor within the hardware.](data:image/png;base64,)

### The use of containers

Lightweight virtualisation that allows applications to run in isolated environments on shared operating system.

Own filesystem, libraries, dependencies

Called virtual execution environment(VEE)

Or Virtual private servers(VPS)

Or Containers

Benefit

- Min startup/shutdown times
- Low resource requirement,
- Highly scalability

VM and host can synchronise state changes if required

Disadvantage all VMs must be on the same VM

![The virtualisation layer is inserted inside an OS to partition the hardware resources for multiple VMs to run their applications in virtual environments.](data:image/png;base64,)

### Resource provisioning methods

To ensure performance and QoS need to manage hardware, CPI, storage, networks, software like load balancers and database server management systems,

Resources can be adjusted by spinning up or shutting down VMs according to demand.

Methods

- Demand driven resource provisioning. Based on current utilisation and thresholds.
- Evend driven resource provisioning. Based on specific time event.
- Popularity-driven resource provisioning: Predict increase in traffic based on popularity.

### Popular cloud providers

AWS

Core architecture

- EC2 Compute Engine
- S3 Storage Cloud
- Elastic load balancing and autoscaling is enabled by CloudWatch

Big data handling

- Elastic MapReduce(EMR) is equivalent to Hadoop.
- AWS Import/Exports allows you to ship hardrives.
- CloudFront implements a content distribution network to provide a globally distributed network of proxy servers to cache content.

Payment systems

- Amazon DevPay is a simple to use online billing and account management service. Flexible Payments Service provides developers of commercial systems on AWS with a convenient way to charge customers
- Fulfilment Web Services allows merchants to access Amazon Cloud through simple Web services.

Compute services

- EC2 supports many Cloud services with a cluster of machine instances - Amazon Machine Images(AMI) offers templates to create machine instances of various types.

Storage services

- S3 is key-value. Create bucket, upload to bucket, permissions.

Machine learning

- Amazon SageMaker, create, train and deploy machine-learning models

Google Cloud Platform(GCP)

- Google App Engine(like EC2)
- Big Query(Map reduction like Hadoop)
- Google Compute Engine - raw infrastructure
- Google Cloud Datastore - NoSQL database
- Google Cloud Storage - Object Storage
- Google Cloud SQL

Google search engine

Google Application Engine(GAE). Managed platform with built-in security and autoscaling. Select how many CPUs, RAM and disk storage

- Datastore - object-orientated, distributed, based on Big Table techniques
- Application runtime environment offers a platform for scalable web programming and execution. Supports python and java.
- Software development kit
- Admin console
- GAE web service infrastructure to manage storage and network resources

Machine Learning

- Google Cloud vision
- Google cloud speech
- Google Natural Language
- Google Cloud Translate

Tensor Processing Units

- Open source tensor analysis - matrix calculus.
- Tensor Processing Units(TPUs) are specifically designed to do only Tensor calculations.

Alpha Go was powered by TPUs.

Microsoft Azure

Cloud layer

- Computer
- Mobile
- Web
- Storage
- Analytics
- Networking
- Media and content delivery
- Databases
- Migration
- Intune

AzureML

- Azure ML studio. GUI
- AutoML creates several piplines in parallel and try different algorithms and parameters.

IBM Cloud

Gaining headway in the financial service sector.

Known for bare metal.
